pages.text:
FULL POWER

I invest Jack B. Kubisch, Chargê d'Affaires ad interim of the United States of America to the French Republic, with full power and authority for and in the name of the United

States of America to sign the Protocol amending the Convention signed at Paris on November 22, 1928, concerning International

Expositions, together with the Customs Annex to the Protocol, subject to a reservation with respect to paragraph (2) of

Article 10 of the Convention appended to the Protocol, the said

Protocol with Annex to be transmitted to the President of the

United States of America for ratification by and with the advice and consent of the Senate of the United states of America.

IN TESTIMONY WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Department of State to be affixed, at the city of Washington, in the District of Columbia, this twenty-eighth day of November, 1972.

The Chargé d'Affaires ad interim of the United

States of America presents his compliments to His

Excellency the Minister for Foreign Affairs of the French Republic and has the honor to transmit herewith the full power accorded by the Secretary of State of the United States to the Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, to sign the Protocol amending the Convention signed at paris on

November 22, 1928, concerning International

Exhibitions.

Enclosure:

Full Power to Sign issued by Secretary of State

Embassy of the United States of America
